From Listing — Why This Works:
Rare low 4.625% rate locked in well below today’s market. Seller carryback at just $220/mo keeps payments manageable. Strong entry point for a coastal rental or long-term hold. Easy access to Jacksonville and major highways. Minutes from historic downtown St. Augustine. Close to beaches and year-round rental demand.

Note: This is an assignment of contract through NexKey (Sahil). The $35K down payment goes to the wholesaler. The seller carryback of $22,278 at $220/mo is part of the seller’s asking terms — the gap between purchase price and (mortgage + down payment).

The Simple Answer:

5 rooms = $660/mo cash flow. Deal works at every room count.
6 rooms = the deal works. $501/mo cash flow, 28% TWR. This is the minimum you need.
7 rooms = home run. $986/mo cash flow, 42% TWR. If the layout supports it, this is a great deal.

What to do on the walk: Measure the rooms. Can you fit 6 rooms minimum in 1,722 sqft? If yes, this deal is a go at asking terms. If you can get 7, even better.

What Works for Me

Counter Terms · Carryback Scenarios · TWR Comparison

Seller’s Asking Terms

Purchase Price$328,906
Assignment Fee (to Sahil)$35,000
Seller Carryback$22,278 @ $220/mo
Carryback Term101 months (8.4 yrs)
Monthly PITI$1,941
Total Monthly Debt$2,161
Cash to Close~$41,000
6-Room CF / TWR+$1,237/mo · 36.2%

My Counter Terms

Purchase Price$328,906 (unchanged)
Assignment Fee (to Sahil)$15,000 (reduced)
Seller Carryback$22,278 @ $350/mo
Carryback Term64 months (5.3 yrs)
Monthly PITI$1,941
Total Monthly Debt$2,291 (+$130)
Cash to Close~$21,000 (saves $20K)
6-Room CF / TWR+$1,107/mo · 63.3%
Counter Logic: Cut Sahil’s assignment from $35K to $15K, and offer the seller $350/mo on the carryback instead of $220. Seller gets paid faster (5.3 yrs vs 8.4 yrs) — that’s a win for them. Entry drops from $41K to $21K. Monthly goes up $130 but at $200/wk rooms the deal prints money at every room count.
Asking Terms vs Counter Terms by Room Count
Scenario CF (Asking)
$35K + $220/mo		 TWR (Asking)
$41K entry		 CF (Counter)
$15K + $350/mo		 TWR (Counter)
$21K entry
5 Rooms +$660/mo 19.3% +$530/mo 30.3%
6 Rooms +$1,237/mo 36.2% +$1,107/mo 63.3%
7 Rooms +$1,815/mo 53.1% +$1,685/mo 96.3%
Assumptions: $200/wk per room + 1 private @ $250/wk, 85% occupancy, self-managed, utilities owner-paid. Asking entry = $35K + ~$6K CC = $41K. Counter entry = $15K + ~$6K CC = $21K. Carryback $22,278, 0% interest. TWR = annual CF ÷ cash invested.
Key Finding: At $200/wk rooms, this deal prints at every room count — even at full asking. Counter drops entry from $41K to $21K while giving the seller a faster payoff ($350/mo = 5.3 yrs vs $220/mo = 8.4 yrs). At 6 rooms, counter hits 63.3% TWR. The monthly CF drops $130 vs asking but the TWR nearly doubles because you’re in for $20K less.

Negotiation Strategy

1
Lead with assignment fee reduction
“Sahil, I’m interested but $35K assignment is steep for a sub-to with carryback. I can close fast and clean at $15K. I’ll also bump the seller’s carryback to $350/mo — they get paid off in 5 years instead of 8. Win for them, works for me.”
2
Seller wins too: faster payoff
“The seller gets the full $22,278, just at $350/mo instead of $220. That’s faster for them — done in 5 years, not 8. And I’m putting $15K down to Sahil today. Everybody wins.”
3
Best realistic outcome: $15K down + $350/mo carryback
Entry drops to $21K. At 6 rooms: +$1,107/mo cash flow, 63.3% TWR. Seller gets full $22,278 in 5.3 years. Everyone wins.
4
Floor: Asking terms still strong at 6+ rooms
Even at full asking ($35K + $220/mo), 6 rooms hits +$1,237/mo and 36.2% TWR. The deal is strong either way. Counter just makes it a home run — don’t walk over assignment fee if the house supports 6 rooms.

Risk Flags & Next Steps

Due Diligence · Location · Decision Checklist
4.625% Rate Lock
Locked well below today’s 7%+ market. $271K at 4.625% vs 7% saves $450/mo in interest. This is the real asset.
No HOA · No Flood Insurance
Zone X flood zone. No HOA restrictions on room rental model. Clean for conversion.
Metal Roof
Long lifespan, lower insurance premiums. No near-term roof CapEx.
St. Augustine Location
Year-round rental demand. Tourism, beaches, Jacksonville commuters. Strong weekly rental market.
Room Count Unknown Until Walk
1,722 sqft should support 6-7 rooms but layout determines everything. Must walk before committing.
Assignment of Contract
NexKey wholesaler. Cannot contact seller directly. All negotiation through Sahil.
SOH Tax Reset
Save Our Homes exemption resets on transfer. Taxes will increase from ~$2,598/yr to ~$4,800/yr (+$183/mo). Already factored into PITI.
Due-on-Sale Risk (Standard SubTo)
Standard subject-to risk. Mitigate with land trust. Low probability of call with current rates.

Next Steps — Decision Checklist

1
Call Sahil — (786) 969-9945
Discuss counter: $15K assignment + $350/mo carryback. Seller gets paid faster (5.3 yrs). Entry drops from $41K to $21K.
2
Schedule Walk
Measure rooms. Can you fit 6 bedrooms minimum? Check bathroom count, closet sizes, natural light. Verify turn-key condition.
3
Run OR Lien Search + Code Enforcement
St. Johns County official records. Check for liens, code violations, pending actions.
4
Verify Mortgage Current Status
Confirm mortgage is current, no arrears. Get payoff statement or recent statement.
5
Decision: Go/No-Go After Walk
If 6+ rooms feasible → deal is a home run at asking (36.2% TWR) or counter (63.3% TWR). Even 5 rooms works (+$530-660/mo). Go unless walk-through reveals structural issues.
